heading: Fees relating to applications for adjustment of status
label: 1812 Fees relating to applications for adjustment of status
notes: References in Text, referred to in subsec. (g)(3)(B), was repealed by , , . See .Section 1252b(c)(3)(B) of this titlePub. L. 104–208, div. C, title III, § 308(b)(6)110 Stat. 3009–615section 1229a(b)(5)(C)(ii) of this titleSept. 30, 1996

children.heading: Fee for filing an application to adjust status to that of a lawful permanent resident
children.label: a Fee for filing an application to adjust status to that of a lawful permanent resident

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: In addition to any other fees authorized by law, the Attorney General shall require the payment of a fee, equal to the amount specified in paragraph (2), by any alien who files an application with an immigration court to adjust the alien’s status to that of a lawful permanent resident, or whose application to adjust his or her status to that of a lawful permanent resident is adjudicated in immigration court. Such fee shall be paid at the time such application is filed or before such application is adjudicated by the immigration court.

children.label: ii
children.text: such amount as the Attorney General may establish, by rule.

children.label: i
children.text: the amount of the fee required under this subsection for the most recently concluded fiscal year; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the product resulting from the multiplication of the amount referred to in clause (i) by the percentage (if any) by which the Consumer Price Index for All Urban Consumers for the month of July preceding the date on which such adjustment takes effect exceeds the Consumer Price Index for All Urban Consumers for the same month of the preceding calendar year, rounded to the next lowest multiple of $10.

children.heading: Disposition of adjustment of status application fees
children.label: 3 Disposition of adjustment of status application fees
i
children.text: shall be derived by transfer from the Immigration Examinations Fee Account under ; andsection 1356(n) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: shall be credited to the Executive Office for Immigration Review to retain and spend without further appropriation; and

children.label: B
children.text: any amounts not derived by transfer and credited pursuant to subparagraph (A) shall be deposited into the general fund of the Treasury.

children.heading: Fee for filing application for waiver of grounds of inadmissibility
children.label: b Fee for filing application for waiver of grounds of inadmissibility

children.text: In addition to any other fees authorized by law, the Attorney General shall require the payment of a fee, equal to the amount specified in paragraph (2), by any alien at the time such alien files an application with an immigration court for a waiver of a ground of inadmissibility, or before such application is adjudicated by the immigration court.

children.label: ii

children.heading: Fee for filing an appeal of a decision of an immigration judge
children.label: d Fee for filing an appeal of a decision of an immigration judge

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Except as provided in paragraph (3), the Attorney General shall require, in addition to any other fees authorized by law, the payment of a fee, equal to the amount specified in paragraph (2), by any alien at the time such alien files an appeal from a decision of an immigration judge.

children.label: ii
children.text: such amount as the Attorney General may establish, by rule.

children.label: i
children.text: the amount of the fee required under this subsection for the most recently concluded fiscal year; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the product resulting from the multiplication of the amount referred to in clause (i) by the percentage (if any) by which the Consumer Price Index for All Urban Consumers for the month of July preceding the date on which such adjustment takes effect exceeds the Consumer Price Index for All Urban Consumers for the same month of the preceding calendar year, rounded to the next lowest multiple of $10.

children.heading: Exception
children.label: 3 Exception
children.text: The fee required under paragraph (1) shall not apply to the appeal of a bond decision.

children.heading: Fee for filing a motion to reopen or a motion to reconsider
children.label: g Fee for filing a motion to reopen or a motion to reconsider

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Except as provided in paragraph (3), in addition to any other fees authorized by law, the Attorney General shall require the payment of a fee, equal to the amount specified in paragraph (2), by any alien at the time such alien files a motion to reopen or motion to reconsider a decision of an immigration judge or the Board of Immigration Appeals.

children.label: ii
children.text: such amount as the Attorney General may establish, by rule.

children.label: i

children.label: ii

children.label: A

children.text: a motion to reopen a deportation order entered in absentia if such motion is filed in accordance with prior to .section 1252b(c)(3)(B) of this titleApril 1, 1997

children.label: i Fee for filing application for cancellation of removal for certain permanent residents

children.label: 1 In general
children.text: In addition to any other fees authorized by law, the Attorney General shall require the payment of a fee, equal to the amount specified in paragraph (2), by any alien at the time such alien files an application with an immigration court an application for cancellation of removal for an alien who is a lawful permanent resident.1So in original.1

children.label: j Fee for filing an application for cancellation of removal and adjustment of status for certain nonpermanent residents

children.heading: Limitation on use of funds
children.label: k Limitation on use of funds
children.text: No fees collected pursuant to this section may be expended by the Executive Office for Immigration Review for the Legal Orientation Program, or for any successor program.
